class FenwickTree(val size: Int) {
    private val tree = LongArray(size + 1)
    fun update(pos: Int, delta: Long) {

            i -= i.and(i-1)

    }

    private val tree: MutableList<Int>
    private val size: Int

    constructor(size: Int) {

    }

            i -= i and -i

        return res
    }

    fun rangeQuery(l: Int, r: Int): Int {
        return query(r) - query(l - 1)
    }

class FenwickTree {
    private val tree: MutableList<Int>
    private val size: Int

    constructor(size: Int) {
        this.size = size
        this.tree = MutableList(size + 1) { 0 }
    }

    fun update(index: Int, delta: Int) {
        var i = index + 1 // 1-based index
        while (i <= size) {
            tree[i] += delta
            i += i and -i
        }
    }

    fun query(index: Int): Int {
        var res = 0
        var i = index + 1 // 1-based index
        while (i > 0) {
            res += tree[i]
            i -= i and -i
        }
        return res
    }

    fun rangeQuery(l: Int, r: Int): Int {
        return query(r) - query(l - 1)
    }
}

fun solve(nums: Array<Long>, queries: Array<Array<Long>>): Array<Long> {
    val n = nums.size
    val ft = Array(6) { FenwickTree(n) }

    // Precompute depths for each number
    for (i in 0 until n) {
        val x = nums[i]
        val depth = getDepth(x)
        if (depth <= 5) {
            ft[depth].update(i, 1)
        }
    }

    val answer = Array(queries.size) { 0L }

    for (i in 0 until queries.size) {
        val query = queries[i]
        when (query[0].toInt()) {
            1 -> {
                val l = query[1].toInt()
                val r = query[2].toInt()
                val k = query[3].toInt()
                if (k < 0 || k > 5) {
                    answer[i] = 0L
                } else {
                    answer[i] = ft[k].rangeQuery(l, r).toLong()
                }
            }
            2 -> {
                val idx = query[1].toInt()
                val val = query[2].toLong()
                // Get old value's depth
                val old_val = nums[idx]
                val old_depth = getDepth(old_val)
                if (old_depth <= 5) {
                    ft[old_depth].update(idx, -1)
                }
                // Update nums
                nums[idx] = val
                // Compute new depth
                val new_depth = getDepth(val)
                if (new_depth <= 5) {
                    ft[new_depth].update(idx, 1)
                }
            }
        }
    }

    return answer
}

fun getDepth(x: Long): Int {
    var current = x
    var depth = 0
    while (current != 1L) {
        current = current.bitCount()
        depth++
    }
    return depth
}
